attributes.action: Expand CAMHS assessments to include neurodivergent children in severe distress
attributes.additional_details: Many neurodivergent children experience severe anxiety, emotional dysregulation, school refusal and family breakdown, yet are unable to access CAMHS unless they self-harm or reach crisis. This delays early intervention, increases long-term harm, and places additional pressure on families, schools and emergency services. Early assessment improves outcomes and reduces future costs.
attributes.background: Expand CAMHS assessment criteria so neurodivergent children in severe distress can access timely mental health assessments and support, without needing to self-harm or reach crisis point.

rejection.details:
We understand that you are concerned about how neurodivergent children access CAMHS but we are not sure what you would like to change. 

You may wish to sign this petition which calls for a similar action: https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/759345
